# arbitro

> `@edmolima/arbitro` is a deterministic, offline model router for OpenRouter.
> Given a prompt, `judge()` returns the best model to use — the chosen `ModelEntry`,
> ranked alternatives, an inferred task/complexity, a structured-output flag, and a
> confidence heuristic — with no network calls and no runtime dependencies. You make
> the actual model call yourself using `decision.model.slug`.

Key facts:

- Pure and reproducible: same prompt → same decision. The engine never calls OpenRouter to decide.
- Tunable cost-vs-quality via `createArbitro({ costPreference })` (0 = cheapest, 1 = best).
- `toOpenRouterBody(decision, prompt)` builds the OpenRouter chat-completion request body.
- TypeScript, ESM, Node >= 18. Published to GitHub Packages as `@edmolima/arbitro`.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/edmolima/arbitro/blob/main/README.md): overview, quickstart, API, cost tuning, OpenRouter call.
- [Consuming guide](https://github.com/edmolima/arbitro/blob/main/docs/consuming.md): installing from GitHub Packages (token, .npmrc, CI, troubleshooting).
- [Package README](https://github.com/edmolima/arbitro/blob/main/packages/arbitro/README.md): the library's own usage docs.
- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/edmolima/arbitro/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): versioned changes (current: 0.2.0).

## Contributing

- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/edmolima/arbitro/blob/main/AGENTS.md): agent/dev guide — commands, conventions, and hard boundaries (zero-dep, offline, behavior-preserving).
- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/edmolima/arbitro/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): setup, commands, PR flow, release process.
- [SECURITY](https://github.com/edmolima/arbitro/blob/main/SECURITY.md): how to report vulnerabilities.
